title: Creating / deleting drivers’ home directories is not cleanly/reliably implemented
body:
I'm prefixing this by saying this is all from a perspective of linux environment with kernels 6.8 with ubuntu distro.

Based on https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/e70c309aabae7d35bf386ff0165ef8c6315a5e26/storage/drivers/overlay/overlay.go#L226

If `overlay support` feature isn't already cached on the host (which will happen every boot since it caches in `/run/containers/storage/overlay`, backed by tmpfs)
It will try to test support, on failure it'll delete `home` which in default configurations would be `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` if I'm not mistaken.

But on success, it'll also attempt to delete `home`, before it eventually recreates it
https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/e70c309aabae7d35bf386ff0165ef8c6315a5e26/storage/drivers/overlay/overlay.go#L709-L716

In the XFS project quota libs section it's suggested that we can assign a project id to `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay`
https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/e70c309aabae7d35bf386ff0165ef8c6315a5e26/storage/drivers/quota/projectquota_supported.go#L112-L125

But, if the `home` deletion is successful, doesn't that remove the XFS project quota ID on `home` if the admin set one up before running a workload?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Well, are you actually seeing that _happen_? I see at least one reason why that directory should never be removed . (related: #666 might break that eventually).

Note to self: The history of that logic, removing the driver’s home, is probably 76b3aa849495eb385a4833b075458fb2e9bde4a9 . And on the other hand, https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/680#discussion_r2960417494 discusses other reasons why this design is not ideal.

Ah you're right. I figured out what was stripping it in my environment. Sorry for the bother. FWIW to confirm your suspicion I spun up a brand new VM that's never ran `podman` and ran strace on it. I can see that it makes `l` and `.has-mount-program` under `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` and never removes them so that os.Remove(home) would always fail it seems. Seems a little tricky, but gets the ideal result for me for now.

I do think this is a problematic design — `.has-mount-program` is not _always_ set, and `l` might be going away; so this is something we should clean up; I was just thinking it’s unlikely to manifest _yet_.

Reopening to track this situation.
